seo.title: Semantic Kernel Guardrails for Healthcare
seo.description: Compliance guide for Semantic Kernel agents in healthcare. Meet HIPAA, FDA 21 CFR Part 11 requirements with Vex guardrails.

content.intro: Healthcare teams using Semantic Kernel face heightened risks when integrating LLMs for patient triage, diagnostics, or personalized treatment planning due to the potential for plugin chaining errors, invalid plans, memory hallucinations, and data contamination. These failure modes can expose protected health information (PHI) or generate unsafe recommendations, violating HIPAA and FDA regulations. Vex provides essential guardrails by filtering kernel function results in real-time, ensuring auditable, compliant AI deployments.

complianceRequirements.description: Requires protection of PHI from unauthorized disclosure and mandates audit trails for all data access.
complianceRequirements.howVexHelps: Vex redacts PHI in real-time from Semantic Kernel function outputs and logs all filtering decisions for HIPAA-compliant audits.

complianceRequirements.description: Mandates electronic records be trustworthy, reliable, and equivalent to paper records with audit controls.
complianceRequirements.howVexHelps: Vex validates AI-generated plans against clinical safety rules and maintains immutable audit logs of all kernel executions.

complianceRequirements.description: Strengthens HIPAA with breach notification requirements and increased penalties for PHI mishandling.
complianceRequirements.howVexHelps: Vex automatically detects and blocks PHI leakage across plugin chains, preventing breaches before they occur.

complianceRequirements.description: Requires demonstrated controls over security, availability, processing integrity, confidentiality, and privacy.
complianceRequirements.howVexHelps: Vex provides continuous monitoring and compliance reporting for Semantic Kernel's memory and planner components.

industryRisks.name: Plugin chaining producing cascading errors
industryRisks.description: Medical history plugin feeds unfiltered data to diagnosis plugin, creating dangerous treatment recommendations.
industryRisks.example: Patient allergy data lost in chain → Prescribe penicillin → Anaphylaxis

industryRisks.name: Planner generating invalid execution plans
industryRisks.description: AI planner sequences plugins incorrectly, bypassing de-identification before LLM processing.
industryRisks.example: Raw PHI reaches GPT → HIPAA violation → $1.5M fine

industryRisks.name: Memory recall hallucinating past interactions
industryRisks.description: Kernel memory fabricates patient history, leading to incorrect medication dosage calculations.
industryRisks.example: Hallucinated 'prior reaction' → Wrong dosage → Patient harm

industryRisks.name: Cross-plugin data contamination
industryRisks.description: Patient A data mixes with Patient B records during parallel processing.
industryRisks.example: Wrong patient gets chemotherapy recommendation → Malpractice lawsuit

implementation.title: Install Vex Kernel Filter (Python)
implementation.description: Add Vex as a Semantic Kernel function result filter to catch PHI and validate outputs before clinical use.
implementation.code:
import semantic_kernel as sk
from vex_sdk import VexFilter

# Install: pip install semantic-kernel vex-sdk
kernel = sk.Kernel()

# Healthcare policy: Block PHI, validate plans
healthcare_policy = {
    'pii.redact': True,
    'healthcare.validate_diagnosis': True,
    'audit.log_all': True
}

vex_filter = VexFilter(policy_id='healthcare-vex-policy')

# Add as kernel function filter
@kernel.register_function(
    function_name='triage_patient',
    plugin_name='healthcare',
    result_filter=vex_filter.filter_result
)
def triage_patient(symptoms: str) -> str:
    # Your triage logic here
    return 'High priority - ER'

# All function results automatically filtered

implementation.title: Memory Store Protection (Python)
implementation.description: Secure Semantic Kernel's memory component against hallucination and PHI contamination.
implementation.code:
from semantic_kernel.memory import SemanticTextMemory
from vex_sdk import VexMemoryFilter

# Protect kernel memory from PHI contamination
memory_filter = VexMemoryFilter(policy='healthcare-memory-safe')

memory_store = SemanticTextMemory(
    storage=memory_filter.protected_store(),
    embeddings=your_embedding_gen
)

# All recalls automatically filtered
await memory_store.save_information(
    collection='patient_interactions',
    text='Patient reports chest pain',
    id='visit_123'
)

# Vex redacts PHI on recall
results = await memory_store.search(
    collection='patient_interactions',
    query='chest pain',
    limit=5
)

# Audit trail: all PHI redactions logged

caseStudyScenario.heading: Semantic Kernel in Healthcare: A Scenario
caseStudyScenario.scenario:
Regional Health Network deploys Semantic Kernel for emergency department triage. The system chains plugins: Patient Intake → Medical History Lookup → Symptom Analysis → Triage Priority. Planners dynamically sequence these based on complexity, pulling from memory stores containing 500K+ patient interactions. During peak flu season, the system processes 2,000 patients daily, with parallel execution across 20 GPU nodes.

Without guardrails, a planner bug sequences History Lookup before PHI de-identification, exposing raw SSNs and diagnoses to the LLM. Memory recall hallucinates 'prior anaphylaxis' for non-allergic patients. Cross-plugin contamination mixes pediatric and geriatric dosages. A single bad plan cascades: wrong patient gets high-risk medication classification.
caseStudyScenario.outcome: Vex kernel filters catch 99.7% of PHI exposures in the first week, blocking 1,247 invalid plans. Dosage validation prevents 23 dangerous recommendations. Immutable audit logs satisfy HIPAA and 21 CFR Part 11 during unannounced audit. Clinical review confirms zero patient safety incidents. The hospital avoids $2.3M in potential fines and maintains SOC 2 certification. Triage accuracy improves 18% with validated AI outputs.
